title: Avoid False Connections: - Look for Patterns: Ask Yourself, “is This Really Connected, or
subtitle: Spot Illusory Correlations
description:
To avoid false connections:
- Look for patterns: Ask yourself, “Is this really connected, or just a coincidence?”
- Seek evidence: Check if there’s actual data supporting the relationship.
- Stay objective: Write down facts separately and then compare.

Example: If you think eating bananas always improves your mood, track it for a week to see if the pattern holds.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Name your suspicious connection
howTo.summary: Identify a specific pattern you suspect is true, such as a belief that eating bananas improves your mood. Write it down clearly to make it testable.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Choose one primary metric to track, like mood on a scale of 0-10. Set a time for measurement and ensure consistency in your logging.

howTo.title: Run the 7-day experiment
howTo.summary: Collect data daily for a week, logging your primary metric and relevant contexts. Keep the process simple to maintain compliance.

howTo.title: Analyze the results
howTo.summary: After the experiment, compare the average metric on days with and without the suspected cause. Determine if the connection is plausible based on your predefined acceptance rule.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your findings and make informed decisions based on the data collected. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/spot-illusory-correlations/.
